perturbation.original_text: 3.1 EFCA shall: 3.1.1 immediately upon re ceipt of a written reques t from the disclos ing part, return to the disc losing part y all doc uments and materials (and all copies thereof) containing t he disclos ing party’s Confidential Information and certify in writing to the dis closing par ty that it has complied with the requirements of this sub-clause; and 3.1.2 notwithstanding the completi on of any purpose for which any Confidential Informati on has been disclosed or the return of documents and materials as aforesaid, continue to the bound by t he undertakings set out i n clauses 2, 4 and 5.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 EFCA shall: 3.1.1 immediately upon re ceipt of a written reques t from the disclos ing part, return to the disc losing part y all doc uments and materials (and all copies thereof) containing t he disclos ing party’s Proprietary Materials and certify in writing to the dis closing par ty that it has complied with the requirements of this sub-clause; and 3.1.2 notwithstanding the completi on of any purpose for which any Confidential Informati on has been disclosed or the return of documents and materials as aforesaid, continue to the bound by t he undertakings set out i n clauses 2, 4 and 5.
perturbation.explanation: In clause 3.1.1, replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Materials' creates an ambiguity. 'Proprietary Materials' is not defined in the agreement, and now it becomes unclear which items EFCA is obligated to return. Clause 3.1.2 refers back to 'Confidential Information', creating an in-text contradiction, since the previous mention of 'Confidential Information' does not exist anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1.2 notwithstanding the completi on of any purpose for which any Confidential Informati on has been disclosed or the return of documents and materials as aforesaid, continue to the bound by t he undertakings set out i n clauses 2, 4 and 5.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Materials' in clause 3.1.1. 'Proprietary Materials' is not defined, creating an ambiguity. The contradicted text (3.1.2) refers back to 'Confidential Information', which is no longer consistently used in the modified 3.1.1, resulting in an in-text contradiction as the meaning of what needs to be returned is no longer consistent.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 EFCA shall: 4.1.1 not divulge the di sclosing party’s Confidenti al Information, in whole or in part, to any third party; and 4.1.2 make no c ommercial use of t he same or any par t thereof without the prior wr itten consent of the di sclosing party. No twithstanding the 2 foregoing, EFCA shall be entitled to make any disc losure required by law of t he disc losing party’s Confide ntial Information provide d that it gives the disc losing party not less t han two business days’ notice of such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 EFCA shall: 4.1.1 not divulge the di sclosing party’s Trade Secrets, in whole or in part, to any third party; and 4.1.2 make no c ommercial use of t he same or any par t thereof without the prior wr itten consent of the di sclosing party. No twithstanding the 2 foregoing, EFCA shall be entitled to make any disc losure required by law of t he disc losing party’s Confide ntial Information provide d that it gives the disc losing party not less t han two business days’ notice of such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Trade Secrets' in clause 4.1.1, it creates a contradiction with the last sentance. The contract now states EFCA cannot divulge trade secrets but can be legally required to disclose confidential information. If Trade Secrets are not Confidential Information or vice-versa, this limits the protection afforded to them. Where the clause is contradictory and how the term has been perturbed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 EFCA shall: 4.1.1 not divulge the di sclosing party’s Trade Secrets, in whole or in part, to any third party; and 4.1.2 make no c ommercial use of t he same or any par t thereof without the prior wr itten consent of the di sclosing party. No twithstanding the 2 foregoing, EFCA shall be entitled to make any disc losure required by law of t he disc losing party’s Confide ntial Information provide d that it gives the disc losing party not less t han two business days’ notice of such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The change in clause 4.1.1 from 'Confidential Information' to 'Trade Secrets' creates a contradiction. The contract now prohibits divulging 'Trade Secrets' but allows for legal disclosure of 'Confidential Information.' The contradicted section is the same clause. If 'Trade Secrets' are considered a subset of or entirely separate from 'Confidential Information,' this creates an inconsistency: EFCA cannot divulge trade secrets but can be legally required to disclose confidential information. If Trade Secrets are not Confidential Information, the change limits the protection afforded to trade secrets.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement and under standing between the parties in respect of C onfidentia l Information and supersedes all previous agreement s, understandings and undertakings in suc h respect. This Agreement cannot be c hanged except by wr itten agreement between t he parties. The inter pretation, construction and effect of this Agreement shall be enforced in accor danc e with the laws of the State of Victoria. This agree ment will b e effective as of the date below, and contin ue for five (5) years.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding between the parties in respect of Protected Information and supersedes all previous agreement s, understandings and undertakings in suc h respect. This Agreement cannot be c hanged except by wr itten agreement between t he parties. The inter pretation, construction and effect of this Agreement shall be enforced in accor danc e with the laws of the State of Victoria. This agree ment will b e effective as of the date below, and contin ue for five (5) years.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' only in the first sentence, without defining 'Protected Information' anywhere in the agreement, the scope of the entire agreement becomes ambiguous. The rest of the agreement still refers to 'Confidential Information', creating a contradiction: what does the agreement actually cover, 'Confidential Information' or 'Protected Information'? The 'Protected Information' is not defined creating the perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In this Agreement:- Confidential Informa tion means any and all information which is now or at any time disclosed to one party by the other party and shall inc lude without limitation, data, know-how, fo rmulae, recipes, processes, designs and des ign rights, photogr aphs, drawings, plans, intentions, product information, trade secrets, market opportunities, specifications , manufacturing data, which is disc losed to EF CA before or after the date of this Agreement but shall exc lude any part of such information which:- 1.1 is or bec omes in the public domain without breach of this Agreement by EFCA; or 1.2 EFCA can show:- 1 1.2.1 was in its possession or known to it by being in its use or being recorded in its files or computers or other recording m edia prior to receipt from the dis closing pa rty and which was not previously acquired by EFCA from the discl osing party under an obligation or confidence, or 1.2.2 to have been developed by EF CA, or EF CA’s principals, at any time independently of any info rmation disclosed to it by the disclos ing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text referred to 'Confidential Information,' which was clearly defined in the 'Contradicted Text' section. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Information' in the first sentence, but 'Protected Information' is never defined. This creates a contradiction because the rest of the agreement, including the 'Contradicted Text' section, still refers to 'Confidential Information.' It is now unclear whether the agreement covers only 'Confidential Information' as defined, or also some undefined 'Protected Information,' leading to a Misaligned Terminology in-text contradiction as described in the definition.
